findings.title: SQL injection via unsanitized user input in search endpoint
findings.code_snippet:
query = f"SELECT * FROM products WHERE name LIKE '%{user_input}%'"
cursor.execute(query)
findings.evidence: user_input is taken from request.args without any sanitization or parameterization; attacker can inject OR 1=1 to dump entire table.
findings.action: Use parameterized query: cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM products WHERE name LIKE %s', (f'%{user_input}%',))

findings.title: Hardcoded API key committed to repository
findings.evidence: The key appears to be live per naming convention; once published to a public repo, rotation is mandatory.
findings.action: Rotate key via Stripe dashboard, move to environment variable, add src/config.py key pattern to pre-commit hook (detect-secrets).

findings.title: JWT signature verification disabled in auth middleware
findings.evidence: Signature verification is explicitly disabled; any attacker can forge a token with arbitrary claims.
findings.action: Remove the options dict; pass public key/secret and algorithms=['RS256'] explicitly.

ideas.title: Adopt bandit + semgrep in CI for static security scanning
ideas.detail: Run bandit on every PR for Python codebase + semgrep with community rulesets. Fails CI on Medium+. Catches ~80% of OWASP Top 10 drift before it hits main.

ideas.title: Introduce rate limiting on auth endpoints
ideas.detail: Currently /login accepts unlimited requests. Redis-backed token bucket per IP (5 req/min) reduces credential stuffing exposure. Use Flask-Limiter or similar.
